description: Tasks rewritten to match actual generated data

tasks.instruction: Find a maize SNP genotyping panel with at least 50,000 markers, a price per sample of no more than $25, and a rating of 4.5 stars or higher, then add 2 units of that panel to your cart.

tasks.instruction: Configure a breeding management software plan for 12 users that includes both field trial management and genomic selection modules with monthly billing, then choose the cheapest option that costs at or below $600 per month and save the configuration.

tasks.instruction: For wheat targeted sequencing of 96 samples, compare at least two services and then choose one that offers a turnaround time of 21 days or less and a price under $80 per sample, adding an order for 96 samples to your quote.

tasks.instruction: Add three rice-focused genomic selection training courses at Intermediate or Advanced level, each lasting no more than 2 days, to a learning plan.

tasks.instruction: Create a new sorghum breeding project named “Drought_Trial_2026” for the 2026 season that targets both drought tolerance and grain yield, and assign it to exactly two field locations.

tasks.instruction: Use the marker-assisted selection ROI calculator to compare two 5-year scenarios with different costs and expected yield gains, then select and save the scenario that shows the higher 5-year ROI percentage.

tasks.instruction: From the case study library, find maize projects in Africa that report at least a 10% yield increase and add three of them to a reading list named “Africa Maize Impact.”

target_names: Genomic Selection Delivers 18% Maize Yield Gain in Western Kenya
Marker-Assisted Selection Boosts Maize Yields by 12% in Northern Nigeria
Drought-Tolerant Maize Hybrids Deliver 25% Yield Increase in Eastern Ethiopia
